timeline.title: An unrelated project
timeline.preview: Why I locked myself in my room for 7 days
timeline.fullStory:
In grade 11, our school made us put together a capstone project. It could be about anything, they said. We had a year to prepare, and would be presenting it in the first month of grade 12. Being the person I am given the freedom I got, I procrastinated on this project. I would start during the summer *after* grade 11. With the deadline looming, I was getting desperate. So I went with the first idea that popped into my head that random summer day: I would lock myself in my room without my phone for 7 days. Okay, maybe not lock myself in my room. But I would do a digital detox, and film the entire process. And thats exactly what I did. It would be first ever personal project I ever made. It would also be the first time I ever tried journaling (that’s important for the story later on).

I also made a video for it: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=csKyTxwM5GA

timeline.title: The spark
timeline.fullStory: Fast forward 1 year later. I graduated. Yay! But now it was summer, and I had nothing to do. What better time to start a project? So that’s what I did. I began by doing some initial brainstorming. Here were some of the initial ideas I had. If you look really closely, you can pinpoint the exact moment I decided on doing a project on happiness (hint: it's “happiness” in bolded letters).

timeline.title: Messy frantic writing
timeline.fullStory: This was the very first happiness journal I ever wrote. After some time spent thinking about how to actually learn more about happiness, I settled on something simple: I would keep a journal. So, for the entirety of my trip in China (37 days), I set around 2 hours each day to write about what made me happiest that day. Most of my evenings were spent writing. Some would days it would be on a table in a study or room. Some would would be on the dining table of whichever relatives’ house were were staying at. Once it was on a train.

timeline.title: Sticky notes on the wall
timeline.preview: Deciphering what I wrote and making sense of it all
timeline.fullStory:
After a lot of writing, I preceded to spend a lot of time reading… mostly what I wrote. With all the entries in front of me, I now had to find a way to string them all together, and discover the underlying theme buried under the many lines of ink. To structure this process a bit more, I decided to use sticky notes. Gradually, my wall would resemble those conspiracy theory walls as I began making connections between the individual insights of each sticky note. Once the themes began to surface above the multi-coloured jumble of squares, I went back to writing. Around the same time, I also started talking to some friends about the project, having some amazing conversations about happiness. My writing, combined with these conversations, would form the basis of the final script I would use to make the documentary. 

Also, I couldn’t find pictures of the sticky notes because why would I take pictures of sticky notes so this is a picture of the desk in China where I wrote a lot of my journal entries. The core ideas I later talked about in the documentary would be first formed here.

timeline.title: Un-alienated labor
timeline.preview: Realizing that Marx had a point
timeline.fullStory:
After 138 pages of journals, tens of hours of footage, and more than 7 hours of recorded conversations with friends, here is my best attempt at answering the question: What is Happiness? 

It was a culmination of 7 months of planning and dedication. To be honest, I didn’t think I would even end up finishing it. I still remember the moment I uploaded the video. I think, perhaps for the first time, I fully understood what Marx meant when he talked about the un-alienated labor. To produce something that was entirely intrinsically mine, free from any external ulterior motivations truly did feel different than any other thing I had ever made. There really was a tangible difference. 

Here’s the documentary: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Rt-2zGdUv00

timeline.title: Sub Stack?
timeline.preview: The inevitable intersection where all aspiring writers meet in 2026
timeline.fullStory:
When I initially posted the documentary, my intention was to continue this project by making a podcast sometime in the near future. And then one day, I saw someone post a story about a Substack article their friend wrote. And in that moment, something clicked. I had a bunch of journals I had written. Some that I thought were worth sharing. Others I wanted to expand on. 

I could start a Substack. 

I remember turning the idea around in my head at first. When it came time to write my monthly reflection for February, I mentioned the idea. “Definitely a project to start out for the summer.” I said. After writing that I wondered why I couldn’t start right then and there. And realizing I could, that’s what I did. That’s why the first article was published on March 1st.

timeline.title: Small piano room
timeline.preview: An interesting turn of events that led me to an interesting little room
timeline.fullStory:
As the thought of summer break began to be more and more of a foreseeable reality, I began daydreaming. I imagined what the summer could look like. I had 4 months all to myself in the city. There were so many possibilities. As my time spent daydreaming increased, so did my ambitions for this project. I no longer just wanted to make a podcast and write articles, I wanted to do more. 

And that’s when I broke my arm. 

Laying on the hospital bed, all those plans I had daydreamed about came crashing down. All of a sudden, the idea of “many possibilities” did not seem as enticing. 

However, as things began to calm down and I began to heal, the daydreams gradually began to come back. So much so that in less than 2 weeks since breaking my arm, I found myself in a small piano room downstairs in our residence, sitting beside a circular table, planning my summer once more. 

One of my ideas was to make this website, and I was buying remember buying the domain for it that day.

timeline.title: (summer) Project 2026 (not political)
timeline.preview: Continuing the tradition of doing something in the summer
timeline.fullStory:
After a lot of planning and daydreaming, I finally released the first video of the summer. I had no idea what would happen in the next 4 months, but I was excited to find out. It would be my 3rd consecutive year of taking on a personal project like this, and honestly, it was just nice to be in that feeling of curiosity and exploration again. 

There were 5 main goals in the initial plan: 
1. Start the podcast and have a philosophy professor as a guest 
2. Make a website 
3. Have an article featured on a literary magazine 
4. Go to an event and give a speech about the project 
5. Create a happiness-related app 

Here's a playlist of all the videos: https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLoL0F-5h5HDc3TMtzrp3LAnWaVik7l6HG
